FLOW.COMMENT: sanityCheck - check that this instance is one of our normal configured instances

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: sanityCheck - check that this instance is configured correctly

INVOKE.COMMENT: %csutilBaseFolder% framework - check that the /csutil/framework folder exists
%stack%: Framework working folder '%csutilBaseFolder%/framework' does not exist or is mis-configured!

Values.value: %stack%: Framework working folder '%csutilBaseFolder%/framework' does not exist or is mis-configured!

EXIT.FAILURE-MESSAGE: Framework working folder '%csutilBaseFolder%/framework' does not exist or is mis-configured!
EXIT.COMMENT: Framework working folder '%csutilBaseFolder%/framework' does not exist or is mis-configured!

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: --- check that the hostname:port matches our instance setting ---

INVOKE.COMMENT: %stack%: Framework configuration not valid: thisinst '%thisinst%' does not match instance '%instance%'!

Values.value: %stack%: Framework configuration not valid: thisinst '%thisinst%' does not match instance '%instance%'!

EXIT.FAILURE-MESSAGE: Framework configuration not valid: thisinst '%thisinst%' does not match instance '%instance%'!
EXIT.COMMENT: Framework configuration not valid: thisinst '%thisinst%' does not match instance '%instance%'!

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: --- check that the idAllocator is scheduled and not suspended ---

INVOKE.COMMENT: %stack%: Framework ID Allocator service is not scheduled to run!

Values.value: %stack%: Framework ID Allocator service is not scheduled to run!

EXIT.FAILURE-MESSAGE: Framework ID Allocator service is not scheduled to run!
EXIT.COMMENT: Framework ID Allocator service is not scheduled to run!
